goal: Root-cause the cuSolver IllegalAddress crash at step 12000 and relaunch training before Raj goes to bed.
thoughts: cudaErrorIllegalAddress in contact reduction at ~12k steps is almost always contact-buffer overflow from too many simultaneous contacts — direct consequence of num_envs=4096. Drop to 2048 first (cheapest fix), then bump gpu_max_rigid_contact_count and gpu_max_particle_contacts if needed. Not a driver bug.
code:
explain_error(error_text="CUDA error in cuSolverContactReduction: cudaErrorIllegalAddress")

diagnose_physics_error(log_tail="<LAST_200_LINES>")

# Fix: halve num_envs AND raise contact buffers
set_physics_params(
    scene_path="/physicsScene",
    gpu_max_rigid_contact_count=<1048576>,        # default 524288
    gpu_max_num_partitions=<8>,
    gpu_collision_stack_size=<67108864>
)

launch_training(
    task="<ISAAC_LAB_TASK>",
    num_envs=<2048>,                                # was 4096
    headless=True,
    log_dir="<ABS_LOG_DIR>",
    resume=True,                                    # keep progress
    checkpoint="<LATEST_CHECKPOINT>"
)

# Fallback if it recurs: disable GPU pipeline for this task — CPU contacts, slower but stable.
failure_modes: Catching the exception and continuing — PhysX state is already corrupt
Raising contact buffers without lowering num_envs — still OOMs
Resuming from a checkpoint captured post-corruption
